skill.name: pptx-posters
skill.description: Generate PowerPoint presentations and academic posters from paper abstracts or full paper content, with automatic layout optimization and citation formatting.
skill.summary: Generate PowerPoint presentations and academic posters from paper abstracts or full paper content with automatic layout optimization.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: After AI review, all 51 static analysis findings are determined to be false positives. The 28 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags matched on non-cryptographic content in markdown documentation and an audit results JSON file; no cryptographic primitives exist in the code. The 17 external command flags are documentation examples in markdown code blocks, not executable shell commands. The single file write operation at scripts/main.py:187 is an intentional feature that writes user-requested output to disk. No malicious code, data exfiltration, credential harvesting, or prompt injection was detected. The skill is safe to publish with a LOW risk level.

low_findings.title: File write operation to user-specified path
low_findings.description: scripts/main.py line 187 opens a user-provided file path for writing generated python-pptx code. This is an intentional feature requiring the --generate-code flag. The output path is controlled by the user via --output argument. No path traversal or arbitrary file overwrite vulnerability exists since only generated .py files are written.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in skill documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 7 contains a GitHub repository URL for source attribution. This is a documentation reference, not an active network call. No data exfiltration or unauthorized network access risk.

low_findings.title: Reference to HTTP libraries in audit results file
low_findings.description: The audit_result_v2.json file contains references to Python HTTP libraries as part of its own analysis metadata. These are not actual HTTP calls made by the skill code. The skill makes no network requests during operation.

content.user_title: Generate PowerPoint Posters and Slides from Academic Papers
content.value_statement: Converts paper abstracts and PDFs into structured PowerPoint posters and slide decks with automatic layout optimization and design templates. Researchers can quickly create conference presentations without manual formatting.
actual_capabilities: Generate poster outlines from paper abstracts with classic, column, or modular layouts
Create slide deck outlines with academic, conference, or lightning talk styles
Generate python-pptx source code for automated PowerPoint file creation
Support multiple design templates with custom colors and fonts
Structure research content into standard academic sections
Parse abstract text and extract title, background, methods, results, and conclusion sections
limitations: Does not generate original research content or fabricate study data or figures
Figure placeholders are inserted; actual images must be added manually
Citation formatting follows standard styles but should be verified before final submission
Cannot process encrypted, image-only, or corrupt PDF files

use_cases.title: Conference Poster Creation
use_cases.description: Convert a paper abstract or full PDF into a professional academic poster with classic section layout for conference presentation.
use_cases.target_user: PhD student or postdoctoral researcher preparing a conference poster

use_cases.title: Research Presentation Generation
use_cases.description: Input a research paper and produce a structured slide deck with background, methods, results, and conclusion sections.
use_cases.target_user: Researcher preparing a lab meeting or symposium talk

use_cases.title: Automated Presentation Pipeline
use_cases.description: Use the python-pptx code generation feature to build custom presentation automation workflows for batch processing of papers.
use_cases.target_user: Developer or research group automating presentation generation

prompt_templates.title: Create a Poster from an Abstract
prompt_templates.prompt: I have this paper abstract: [paste abstract]. Please convert it into an academic poster using the classic layout.
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic poster generation from text abstract

prompt_templates.title: Generate Slides from a Paper
prompt_templates.prompt: Here is my research paper: [paste content]. Please create a conference-style presentation with slides covering the hook, problem, approach, key result, and impact.

prompt_templates.title: Format with Specific Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert the following abstract into a minimal-style poster using the column layout: [paste abstract].

prompt_templates.title: Generate python-pptx Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Read this paper PDF at [path] and generate python-pptx code to create a colorful academic presentation. Save the output to output.pptx.
prompt_templates.scenario: Code generation for automated PPTX creation

output_examples.input: Abstract text describing a novel machine learning approach for medical image segmentation
output_examples.output: Academic poster outline with sections: Title, Abstract, Introduction, Methods, Results, Conclusion, References. Template uses Arial Bold for titles, primary color #003366, and classic column layout.

output_examples.input: Full PDF of a cancer immunotherapy research paper for a conference lightning talk
output_examples.output: Lightning talk slide outline: Title slide with paper information, one summary slide with key findings. Minimal template with Helvetica font, black primary color, and light gray background.

best_practices: Provide the complete abstract or full paper text to generate the most detailed and accurate outline
Specify the output format (poster or slides) and template preference explicitly for best results
Review all generated content, especially citation formatting, before final submission
anti_patterns: Asking the skill to write original research content or fabricate study data from scratch
Expecting the skill to extract actual figures and charts from PDF files or create data visualizations
Using the skill without providing sufficient source content, resulting in generic or placeholder-heavy output

faq.question: What input formats does this skill accept?
faq.answer: This skill accepts paper abstracts as text and full paper PDFs as input.

faq.question: Can this skill create actual PPTX files?
faq.answer: It generates python-pptx source code that can create PPTX files, and provides detailed layout outlines for manual creation.

faq.question: Does this skill create figures and charts?
faq.answer: No. It creates figure placeholders only. Actual figures must be added manually.

faq.question: What design templates are available?
faq.answer: Three templates are available: academic, minimal, and colorful, each with different color schemes and fonts.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for non-academic content?
faq.answer: It is designed for academic papers, but it can generate slide outlines for any structured text content.

faq.question: What happens if I provide an encrypted PDF?
faq.answer: The skill will report an error and ask you to provide the abstract as text or a readable PDF.
